name: screenpipe
description: screenpipe turns your computer into a personal AI that knows everything you've done. record. search. automate. all local, all private, all yours.

commit.message:
Bump app to v2.2.102

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add Linux x86_64 to release pipeline

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: remove unused publish-release.yml workflow

Publishing is handled by the website admin API, not this workflow.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: drop orphan accessibility_tags table

Created in 20250202 migration but never used in code. The parent
accessibility table was already dropped.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Bump app to v2.2.101

feat: consolidate text search into frames.full_text + frames_fts

Before: 6 FTS tables, accessibility text in 3 places, OCR in 2.
After: frames.full_text is the single searchable text per frame,
frames_fts indexes full_text + app_name + window_name + browser_url.

- Add full_text column to frames, backfill from accessibility_text + ocr_text
- Rebuild frames_fts with clean schema and synchronous triggers
- Drop accessibility, accessibility_fts, ocr_text_fts tables
- Rewrite search_ocr, search_accessibility, count_search_results to use frames_fts
- Update sync_provider and archive to query frames instead of dropped tables
- Disable tree walker (replaced by event-driven capture writing full_text directly)
- Fix search modal: facet FTS alias bug, scroll reset on pagination, keyboard
  results moved above screen grid, app filters use full FTS aggregation
- Clean up all test/bench references to dropped tables

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: enable meeting detection regardless of transcription mode + fix Windows app matching

- Meeting detector now starts unconditionally (was only in batch mode)
- Strip .exe suffix from Windows app names so "Teams.exe" matches "teams"
- Add Windows-specific exe names: ms-teams, chrome, msedge, brave
- Meetings are now detected and persisted to DB even with transcription disabled

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Windows shortcuts (Alt+S/Alt+K) now open overlay instead of home window

Windows was hardcoded to open ShowRewindWindow::Home (the settings/home page)
instead of ShowRewindWindow::Main (the overlay). The overlay infrastructure
already existed for Windows (windows_overlay.rs) but wasn't being used.

Unified show/hide/search shortcut handlers to use main_label_for_mode() on
all platforms instead of hardcoding "home" label on Windows.

Closes #2443

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: skip audio reconciliation when transcription is disabled

Prevents silent-audio deletion path from nuking audio files when
the user has transcription disabled (empty text → delete was triggered).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Bump app to v2.2.100, CLI to v0.3.183

feat: calls filter + collapsible filters on timeline

- Add calls filter row (Phone icon) with one dot per detected meeting
- Click dot to filter timeline to that meeting's frame range
- Hover shows speaker names + time
- Add collapse/expand toggle to hide all filter rows for cleaner view
- Refresh + zoom always visible when collapsed

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: WhatsApp gateway self-terminates when parent dies, timeline forward scroll & fresh end date

- Gateway.mjs monitors stdin EOF + polls parent PID to exit when screenpipe stops (fixes EADDRINUSE on restart)
- Timeline auto-refreshes end date every minute so it doesn't get stuck
- Forward IntersectionObserver fetches newer day data when scrolling left

Bump app to v2.2.99

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Bump app to v2.2.98

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add WhatsApp chat history, contacts sync, and improved API descriptions

- Add /chats endpoint to list recent conversations
- Add /messages?phone=+PHONE&limit=50 endpoint to read chat history
- Fix contacts sync by listening to contacts.update/upsert events
- Store incoming/outgoing messages in memory (last 200 per chat)
- Update connection descriptions to list all available endpoints

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Bump app to v2.2.98

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add Windows ARM64 build and release (#2442)

* feat: Windows ARM64 support (ffmpeg, VC CRT, ONNX Runtime, bun triple)

* feat: Enhance Windows build workflow with ARM64 support

* fix

* fmt

verification.payload:
tree 3932dd7f6d340543796822c08c94f4d39fccd29a

feat: add Windows ARM64 build and release (#2442)

* feat: Windows ARM64 support (ffmpeg, VC CRT, ONNX Runtime, bun triple)

* feat: Enhance Windows build workflow with ARM64 support

* fix

* fmt

commit.message:
Bump app to v2.2.97

- fix: remove Pi agent 60s timeout for long-running tasks
- fix: search timeout 30s→120s for large time range queries
- feat: restore chat filter popover button
- fix: React key collision in filter popover

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
chore: update Cargo.lock

fix: remove Pi agent 60s timeout, increase search timeout to 120s

The 60-second frontend timeout was killing long-running Pi tasks (e.g.
30-day data analysis with 10+ tool calls). Process death is already
detected via pi_terminated event, so the timeout was redundant and harmful.

Search timeout increased from 30s to 120s for large time range queries.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: WhatsApp auto-reconnect on server start + accurate status

- Auto-start WhatsApp gateway on boot if a previous session exists
  (creds.json on disk), so users don't have to manually re-pair after
  every restart
- Fix misleading "connected: true" — now only true when the gateway
  process is actually running, not just when session files exist
- CLI connection list/get now probes localhost:3035 to verify the
  gateway is reachable before reporting connected
- Add which_bun() helper in screenpipe-connect for finding bun path

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Bump app to v2.2.96, CLI to v0.3.182

- fix: Windows overlay focus stealing in fullscreen games
- fix: dense text OCR missed due to aggressive frame downscaling
- fix: auto-update restart delay 5s→30s with warning event
- fix: video exports respect --data-dir (no more C:/F: drive split)
- feat: restore chat filter popover button (lost in merge)
- fix: React key collision in filter popover

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Windows overlay focus stealing, dense text OCR, update delay, exports path

1. Overlay no longer steals focus from fullscreen games (use SWP_NOACTIVATE)
2. Frame comparison downscale factor 6→4 so dense text changes are detected
3. Auto-update restart delay 5s→30s with frontend warning event
4. Video exports use configured data dir instead of hardcoded home_dir

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: drop unused database tables (friend_wearable_requests, ocr_text_embeddings, chunked_text_*)

Remove 5 dead tables that have no inserts or queries in the codebase.
Also remove orphaned DELETE FROM chunked_text_entries in time-range deletion.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Bump app to v2.2.95

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: show skeleton grid while keyword search loads

When UI events loaded faster than keyword search results, the search
modal showed filter chips and keyboard results but no screen thumbnails
(empty grid). Now shows a loading skeleton for the screen section during
this intermediate state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Bump app to v2.2.94, Pi to 0.57.1, bun to 1.3.10

- Pi 0.57.1: strict JSONL framing, dynamic tool registration, offline mode
- Bun 1.3.10 in release workflows
- Fix test compilation: suggestions.rs parse rename, embedded_server missing field

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Bump app to v2.2.93

fix: search shortcut requires two presses on first open

When the overlay wasn't visible, Cmd+Ctrl+K would show the window and
immediately emit open-search, but the webview hadn't mounted its listener
yet. Now delay the emit by 500ms when the window was just created/shown.

Also includes: pi abort/new_session now await RPC responses (no more
sleep-based delays), and minor cleanup from other agents.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: clippy warnings, unused deps, and cargo fmt cleanup

- Replace map(f) on Option with if-let in pi.rs
- Collapse consecutive str::replace calls in text_normalizer.rs
- Prefix unused test variables with underscore
- Remove empty lines after doc comments in test files
- Replace useless vec! with array literals in tests
- Remove unused deps: anyhow (screenpipe-vault), md5 (screenpipe-app)
- Add missing connections field to test PipeConfig initializers
- Run cargo fmt across workspace

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

style: cargo fmt across all crates

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

testing: update spec from recent changes (2026-03-10) (#2439)

Co-authored-by: AI Agent <agent@screenpi.pe>

verification.payload:

committer GitHub <noreply@github.com> 1773159583 -0700

testing: update spec from recent changes (2026-03-10) (#2439)

commit.message:
Bump app to v2.2.92: token counter, fix ShowRewindWindow type, remove duplicate pipe suggestions

- Add token count display to GridDissolveLoader (shows "· 1.2k tokens" during streaming)
- Fix ShowRewindWindow type: Settings → Home to match Rust enum (4 files)
- Remove duplicate "Pipe suggestions" card in general-settings
- Other agent changes: Pi auto-abort, ai-presets, pipes-section improvements

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: auto-abort stuck Pi agent before sending new message

Instead of showing "please wait" toast when the agent is still
processing, abort the previous agent and proceed with the new message.
Also abort before retry on "already processing" error, and reduce
stuck-agent timeout from 180s to 60s.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Bump app to v2.2.91, CLI to v0.3.181

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: search modal click interference from Live Text and wheel handlers

- Remove preventDefault on outer dialog onWheel — was interfering with
  trackpad tap-to-click gestures in WKWebView
- Disable macOS Live Text analysis on search modal images via
  -webkit-image-analysis:none CSS — Live Text creates invisible
  interactive overlays that steal click events from nearby UI elements
- Add data-lm-disable and select-none on thumbnail images

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: hybrid OCR URL detection, search modal UX improvements

- Hybrid OCR: also check browser_url (not just window title) for canvas
  apps. Inside a Google Doc the title is the doc name, not "Google Docs".
- Search modal: hide domain chips when non-browser app is selected
- Search modal: make thumbnail images pointer-events-none to prevent
  macOS Live Text from intercepting clicks
- Search modal: app counts now reflect active date filter so they don't
  change unexpectedly when toggling date chips
- Clear domain filter when selecting an app filter

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Bump app to v2.2.90

fix: Pi crash loop on Windows due to lru-cache ESM/CJS interop under Bun 1.3.7

hosted-git-info requires lru-cache@^10 but bun on Windows hoists lru-cache@7.x
(ESM-only), breaking CJS require(). Seed pi-agent package.json with overrides
to pin the correct version. Also patches existing broken installs on startup.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: meeting detection drops when alt-tabbing during long calls

Two fixes for meetings not being detected when user tabs away from the
meeting app for extended periods (e.g., coding while screen-sharing):

1. Calendar early-join buffer: detect meetings up to 15 min before the
   calendar event start time, since people commonly join early.

2. Audio extension self-sustaining: when bidirectional audio is active
   after a confirmed meeting app, refresh the app confirmation timestamp
   so the 5-min window doesn't expire. Previously, tabbing to a terminal
   for >5 min would kill meeting detection even with active mic+speakers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Bump app to v2.2.89

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: send vocabulary as both prompt and context for OpenAI-compatible STT

mlx-audio (VibeVoice-ASR) expects a `context` form field for hotwords,
not `prompt`. OpenAI Whisper API uses `prompt`. Now sends both fields
so custom vocabulary works regardless of which server is running.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: hybrid OCR capture for canvas-rendered apps (Google Docs, Figma)

Accessibility tree returns only UI chrome (toolbar, sidebar) for canvas-
rendered apps, missing actual document content. Add two-layer detection:

1. Known canvas app patterns (Google Docs, Sheets, Figma, Excalidraw, etc.)
2. Content density heuristic — if <30% of a11y text is from content roles
   (vs AXButton/AXMenuItem chrome), also run OCR

When thin a11y is detected, OCR supplements accessibility data and the
frame is marked text_source="hybrid". Includes 6 unit tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: search modal app filter and date filter timezone bugs

App filter: facet app names came from accessibility table but search
results use frames.app_name — mismatch caused zero filtered results.
Now always derive app chips from actual search results.

Date filter: SQL DATE() bucketed in UTC but labels/results display in
local time, causing off-by-one day mismatches. Now re-buckets facet
dates into local time and compares in local time.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ci): fail release when R2 upload fails

Remove continue-on-error from upload steps and track upload failures
so the job properly fails when artifacts (tar.gz, sig) fail to upload.
Previously, SSL errors on arm64 runner silently skipped the updater
artifact while reporting success.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Bump app to v2.2.88

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ci): prepend MSVC dir to PATH on Windows build step

Git Bash prepends /usr/bin (with GNU link) before MSVC's link.exe.
The tauri-action handled this internally; our shell script needs to
do it explicitly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Bump MCP to v0.9.0

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: chat duplicate processing, panel toggle, and incognito detection

- Filter chat-prefill events by targetWindow so only the chat window processes them (fixes duplicate "already processing" errors)
- Retry piPrompt up to 3x with backoff when Pi reports "already processing"
- Increase abort→newSession→prompt delays for reliable session reset
- Use logical MAIN_PANEL_SHOWN flag for shortcut toggle (fixes invisible panel toggle)
- Batch-query all incognito window titles via AppleScript (fixes non-front-window detection for Arc/Chrome)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(mcp): add resources for pipe creation, API & CLI reference + create-pipe prompt

Exposes screenpipe knowledge to MCP clients:
- screenpipe://pipe-creation-guide - how to build pipes
- screenpipe://api-reference - REST API endpoints
- screenpipe://cli-reference - CLI commands
- create-pipe prompt for guided pipe creation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Update README with Claude integration instructions

Added instructions for using Claude with Screenpipe.

verification.payload:
tree 7e8af6934f88d9cec958aba6aad2bb74a5d01230

author Louis Beaumont <louis.beaumont@gmail.com> 1773085310 -0700
committer GitHub <noreply@github.com> 1773085310 -0700

Update README with Claude integration instructions

Added instructions for using Claude with Screenpipe.

commit.message:
fix: TDZ crash in AI diagnostics and Anthropic provider connection test

- Move isChatGpt declaration to top of runDiagnostics to fix "Cannot access 'v' before initialization" crash
- Skip /v1/models endpoint for Anthropic (unreliable for many keys), validate via chat test instead

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Bump app to v2.2.87

- Fix help tooltip positioning (inline CSS, no Radix portal)
- Pipe optimize/create now sends to home chat instead of overlay
- sessionStorage prefill for same-window navigation
- New connections: airtable, github issues, hubspot, jira, logseq, ntfy, pushover, toggl
- Archive & cloud blob migration

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: help tooltip renders inline (no portal) to fix positioning in scroll containers

Radix Portal teleports content to <body>, breaking position in the
scrollable settings panel. Render PopoverContent without Portal so
it stays within the scroll container's DOM context.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: help tooltip positioning — use bottom/start to avoid drift

side="top" caused Radix collision detection to push tooltips far
from the ? icon when near viewport edges. bottom/start anchors
them consistently below the trigger.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: type error in privacy-section ignoreIncognitoWindows Switch

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add "optimize" button to pipe dropdown menu

Opens Pi chat with a prompt that reads the pipe.md, checks recent
execution logs, and guides the user through improving the pipe prompt
with specific prompt engineering best practices.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add UI toggle for incognito detection and wire through config pipeline

- Add `ignoreIncognitoWindows` boolean to SettingsStore (default: true)
- Wire through: SettingsStore → RecordingConfig → VisionManagerConfig →
  TreeWalkerConfig → platform tree walkers
- Add toggle in privacy-section.tsx under the Redaction section
- Tree walkers now respect the toggle (when false, incognito detection
  is skipped entirely)
- CLI defaults to true (always detect incognito)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ci): smart retry on transient codesign failures in app release

Replace tauri-action with shell script that:
- Re-unlocks macOS keychain before each retry attempt
- Only retries on codesign/bundle transient errors (not compile failures)
- Cleans only bundle output between retries (cargo cache stays warm)
- Exponential backoff (30s, 60s, 90s)
- Up to 3 attempts per platform

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: remove beta build from app release CI

Beta builds are unused — removes Windows, macOS beta rebuild steps
and all should_beta references to save CI time.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(a11y): add robust incognito/private browsing window detection

Replace scattered hardcoded title checks with a dedicated incognito
detection module supporting 20+ locales and macOS AppleScript-based
detection for Chromium browsers (locale-independent, 100% reliable).

- New `crates/screenpipe-a11y/src/incognito/` module with trait-based
  platform abstraction (IncognitoDetector trait + factory)
- macOS: AppleScript `get mode of front window` for Chrome/Edge/Brave/Arc
  with 2s cache and app-not-running guard
- All platforms: localized title matching for 20+ languages (EN, DE, FR,
  ES, PT, IT, NL, PL, TR, RU, UK, AR, HI, TH, VI, CS, RO, HU, SV, NO,
  DA, FI, EL, HE, JA, ZH, KO)
- 40 unit tests covering all languages, edge cases, cache behavior
- Removed duplicated SENSITIVE_TITLES constants from tree walkers
- Removed redundant "private"/"incognito" patterns from config defaults

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add recommended model hints for Ollama provider

Show qwen3.5:9b, glm-4.7:9b, qwen3.5:4b as recommended models with
tool calling support when Ollama is selected. Warn about GPU requirement
and suggest screenpipe cloud/groq as alternatives. Update outdated
ministral placeholder.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Bump app to v2.2.86, CLI to v0.3.180

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: obsidian connection, pipe connections dropdown, scroll/UX fixes

- Add Obsidian as a new connection type (vault_path + daily_note_folder)
- Add connections field to pipe config with dropdown UI
- Add "try in chat" button on connected integrations
- Add connection panel scroll-into-view on selection
- Fix WKWebView scroll swizzle breaking CSS overflow in settings
- Fix duplicate chat-prefill processing guard
- Add make/n8n/zapier connections
- Update obsidian-sync pipe to use connections API
- Various UX improvements (tooltip position, badge navigation)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: prevent duplicate chat-prefill processing

The chat-prefill listener re-subscribes when piInfo changes. During the
brief overlap (async unlisten hasn't resolved), both old and new
listeners receive the same event, causing duplicate abort/session/prompt
sequences and "Agent is already processing" errors.

Add a ref guard (prefillInFlightRef) so only the first listener instance
processes the autoSend flow.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: null-safe navigateToSearchResultRef.current calls in use-scroll-zoom

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Bump app to v2.2.85

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: update Cargo.lock

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
perf(calendar): fetch ICS feeds in parallel and deduplicate events (#2390)

fix: missing closing bracket on div tag in timeline.tsx

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Bump app to v2.2.84

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: resolve merge conflict in general-settings.tsx

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(windows): use Settings window instead of overlay, fix shortcut toggle

- show_main_window/hide_main_window use ShowRewindWindow::Settings on
  Windows (embedded timeline in normal taskbar window, not fullscreen overlay)
- Shortcut toggle checks is_minimized() since Windows close() minimizes
  and minimized windows still report is_visible()==true
- Toggle looks for "settings" label instead of overlay "main" label
- Settings close() minimizes on Windows (stays in taskbar) instead of destroying
- Settings show() calls unminimize() before set_focus() when restoring

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: allow search modal scroll on Windows/Linux embedded timeline

The useScrollZoom wheel handler was calling preventDefault() on all
wheel events, blocking native CSS overflow scrolling inside the search
modal. Add data-search-modal attribute and include it in the exclusion
list so wheel events pass through to the modal's scrollable containers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: embedded timeline scroll via native event swizzle, bump to v2.2.83

WKWebView in standard Tauri WebviewWindows (settings) consumes trackpad
wheel events at the native level before they reach JavaScript. Fix by
swizzling WKWebView.scrollWheel: to emit "native-scroll" Tauri events,
mirroring the existing magnify gesture approach. For NSPanel windows
(overlay), the original implementation is still called so existing JS
wheel handlers continue to work.

- Swizzle WKWebView scrollWheel: to emit native-scroll Tauri events
- Add native-scroll listener in useScrollZoom for frame navigation
- Add native-scroll listener in SearchModal for results/chip scrolling
- Fix thumbnail-highlight-overlay crash on empty matches array
- Add release-app.yml concurrency group

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Bump app to v2.2.82

- Fix meeting detection: use is_input fallback when speaker_id is null
- Add hide/show captions toggle on subtitle bar
- Clean up unused meeting timeline markers

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Live Text hit regions, Windows overlay shortcut, vault lock detection

- VisionKit: defer analysis application until lt_update_position sets correct
  frame geometry, preventing misaligned hit regions and stale URL clicks
- Remove .dataDetectors from overlay interaction types (text selection only)
- Windows Alt+S now opens overlay (ShowRewindWindow::Main) on all platforms
- VaultManager: detect unlocked state when vault.meta exists but no sentinel
  file and DB is not encrypted (was incorrectly treating as Locked)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: pin tauri-cli to 2.10.0 to fix Windows NSIS bundler panic

v2.10.1 introduced a regression (PR #14662) where "./" resource
destinations in tauri.windows.conf.json produce an empty PathBuf,
causing Path::parent() to return None and panic at nsis/mod.rs:782.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: CLI vault commands work directly with filesystem, no server needed

Rewrote all vault CLI commands (setup, lock, unlock, status) to use
VaultManager directly instead of HTTP API. Works offline, no server
dependency. Removed --port flags.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Bump CLI to v0.3.179
Bump app to v2.2.81

chore: include pending changes from other agents

- Fix shortcut settings race condition (single updateSettings call)
- Fix ignoredWindows default assignment (was overwriting disabledShortcuts)
- Reorder nav: Pipes before Timeline
- Regenerated Tauri bindings (vault commands + lockVaultShortcut)
- Updated Cargo.lock

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: disable vault lock in app UI — CLI-only for now

Comment out all app-side vault integration until UX is polished:
- VaultLockDialog mount in layout
- Lock vault shortcut in settings
- Tray menu item
- Global shortcut registration
- Startup vault check

CLI vault commands (screenpipe vault setup/lock/unlock/status) remain
fully functional.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: fast vault unlock — decrypt DB only, data files in background

vault_unlock was waiting for ALL 50GB of data to decrypt before
returning, causing the UI to hang. Now:
- Verify password + decrypt DB (fast, seconds)
- Remove sentinel immediately
- Return to UI so server can start
- Data files decrypt in background tokio task

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: write vault sentinel before encryption, detect encrypted DB as fallback

- Write .vault_locked sentinel at START of lock (not end) so crash
  mid-encryption still shows unlock dialog on restart
- Fallback: if vault.meta exists and db.sqlite has SPVAULT1 magic header,
  treat as locked even without sentinel file
- Fixes: app tried to start server with encrypted DB after restart

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: skip server start when vault is locked, decrypt via Tauri commands

When vault is locked on startup:
- Skip spawning screenpipe server (encrypted DB would crash it)
- Emit vault-locked-on-startup event to show password dialog
- Frontend uses invoke("vault_unlock") to decrypt without server
- After decrypt, spawnScreenpipe starts server normally

Added Tauri commands vault_status and vault_unlock that work
directly with filesystem (no server dependency).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: stop recording on vault lock, restart on unlock, remove hardcoded shortcut text

- Call stopScreenpipe() before locking to ensure DB/files aren't being written
- Call spawnScreenpipe() after unlock to resume recording
- Replace hardcoded "Cmd+Shift+L" references with generic text

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add configurable vault lock shortcut to settings UI

Register vault lock as a proper global shortcut (Cmd+Shift+L / Ctrl+Shift+L)
configurable in Settings > Shortcuts alongside other hotkeys. Removes
hardcoded tray menu accelerator in favor of the global shortcut system.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: anchor help tooltip close to the icon

Use side=bottom, align=start, collisionPadding=0 so the tooltip
appears directly below the ? icon instead of far away.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add descriptive text to connections help tooltip

Shows "Learn how to find your <field> for this integration" before
the link, instead of just a bare link.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: reduce tooltip offset on connections help icon

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: connections help icon shows tooltip on hover instead of click

The ? icon next to credential fields now shows a hover tooltip with
a "How to get this →" link instead of navigating on click, which was
confusing UX.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: hydration error from Badge (div) inside p tag in sync settings

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: vault lock — encrypt screenpipe data at rest (#2411)

* feat: add screenpipe-vault crate for data-at-rest encryption

Phase 1 of vault lock feature. Provides:
- VaultManager: setup/lock/unlock lifecycle with state tracking
- File encryption: ChaCha20-Poly1305 with SPVAULT1 magic header
- Key management: Argon2id password derivation, master key pattern
- Migration: encrypt/decrypt entire data directory with progress tracking
- Idempotent: skips already-encrypted files (magic header check)

All 6 unit tests passing (crypto roundtrip, wrong key rejection,
file encrypt/decrypt, full setup→lock→unlock lifecycle).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: rewrite vault crypto with streaming chunks, atomic writes, crash recovery

- Streaming encryption: 64KB chunks, constant memory regardless of file size
- Atomic writes: encrypt to .vault_tmp then rename (crash = original intact)
- Crash recovery journal: .vault_journal tracks processed files, resumes on restart
- walkdir crate instead of hand-rolled directory walking
- Key zeroized from shared state immediately on lock, explicit zero on task stack
- Wrong key on decrypt leaves encrypted file intact (atomic write)
- 10 tests: streaming large file, wrong key safety, empty file, double lock, etc

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: wire vault into server, CLI, and tray menu

Server:
- VaultManager added to AppState
- Vault middleware returns 423 Locked on all non-vault endpoints when locked
- Routes: GET /vault/status, POST /vault/lock, /vault/unlock, /vault/setup

CLI:
- `screenpipe vault status` — check vault state
- `screenpipe vault setup` — first-time password setup (no-echo input)
- `screenpipe vault lock` — encrypt all data, stop recording
- `screenpipe vault unlock` — decrypt data with password

Tray:
- "Lock vault" menu item with Cmd+Shift+L shortcut
- Emits vault-lock-requested event for frontend to handle

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add vault lock/unlock frontend UI

- VaultLockDialog: full-screen overlay when locked, setup dialog for first use, progress bar during encryption
- useVaultStore: Zustand store managing vault state, API calls to /vault/* endpoints
- Listens for vault-lock-requested Tauri event from tray menu
- Mounted globally in layout.tsx

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(macos): overlay panel visible on first shortcut press

The panel was created with visible(false) and configured on the main
thread, but order_front_regardless() was never called after creation.
This required a second shortcut press to trigger show_existing_main()
which does call it. Now the creation path mirrors show_existing_main:
save_frontmost_app, order_front_regardless, make_key_window, set
first responder, and remove MoveToActiveSpace.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: enable trackpad/wheel scrolling in embedded search results

The embedded search modal was missing onWheel/onTouchMove handlers
that the overlay mode had. Wheel events propagated to the timeline
container which has overscrollBehavior:none, discarding them silently.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(windows): pin @tauri-apps/cli to 2.10.1 to fix NSIS bundler panic

The ^2.10.1 semver range allowed newer patch versions that introduced
a regression in tauri-bundler's NSIS resource path handling, causing
"Couldn't get parent of target path" panic on Windows builds.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
fix(db): sanitize fts5 queries for app_name/window_name to fix syntax error on dots

Bump app to v2.2.80

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: black screen when snapshot JPG missing from disk

Root cause: fallbackImageUrl memo had useVideoMode gate before the
snapshotFailed check. Since useVideoMode defaults to true and is never
set to false for snapshot frames, the HTTP fallback URL was never
computed. Added early return for failed snapshots before the
useVideoMode check, and signal frame unavailable when HTTP also fails.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: show HTTP fallback when snapshot JPG missing on disk When a snapshot frame file doesn't exist, snapshotFailed is set but the fallback was blocked by !useVideoMode being false. Added snapshotFailed to the render condition so the HTTP/ffmpeg fallback is shown instead of a black screen. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6

readme_html:
what is this?

screenpipe turns your computer into a personal AI that knows everything you've done. record. search. automate. all local, all private, all yours

┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  screen + audio → local storage → ai   │
└─────────────────────────────────────────┘
  • remember everything - never forget what you saw, heard, or did
  • search with ai - find anything using natural language
  • 100% local - your data never leaves your machine
  • open source - inspect, modify, own

install

download the desktop app — one-time purchase, all features, auto-updates

or run the CLI:

npx screenpipe@latest record

then

claude mcp add screenpipe -- npx -y screenpipe-mcp

then ask claude what did i see in the last 5 mins? or summarize today conversations or create a pipe that updates linear every time i work on task X

specs

  • 5-10% cpu usage
  • 0.5-3gb ram
  • ~20gb storage/month
  • works offline

Contributing

See CONTRIBUTING.md for guidelines, maintainers, and how to submit PRs. AI/vibe-coded PRs welcome!

Thanks to all contributors:

About screenpipe

screenpipe is an open source application (MIT license) that continuously captures your screen and audio, creating a searchable, AI-powered memory of everything you do on your computer. All data is stored locally on your device. It is the leading open source alternative to Rewind.ai (now Limitless), Microsoft Recall, Granola, and Otter.ai. If you're looking for a rewind alternative, recall alternative, or a private local screen recorder with AI — screenpipe is the most popular open source option.

  • Website: https://screenpi.pe
  • Documentation: https://docs.screenpi.pe
  • Discord: https://discord.gg/screenpipe
  • License: MIT

Who screenpipe is for

  • Knowledge workers who want to recall anything they've seen or heard on their computer
  • Developers who want to give AI coding assistants (Cursor, Claude Code, Cline, Continue) context about what they're working on
  • Researchers who need to search through large volumes of screen-based information
  • People with ADHD who frequently lose track of tabs, documents, and conversations
  • Remote workers who want automatic meeting transcription and notes
  • Teams & enterprises who want to deploy AI across their organization with deterministic data permissions and central config management (screenpi.pe/team)
  • Anyone who wants a private, local-first alternative to cloud-based AI memory tools

Platform support

Platform Support Installation
macOS (Apple Silicon) ✅ Full support Native .dmg installer
macOS (Intel) ✅ Full support Native .dmg installer
Windows 10/11 ✅ Full support Native .exe installer
Linux ✅ Supported Build from source

Minimum requirements: 8 GB RAM recommended. ~5–10 GB disk space per month. CPU usage typically 5–10% on modern hardware thanks to event-driven capture.

Core features

Event-driven screen capture

Instead of recording every second, screenpipe listens for meaningful events — app switches, clicks, typing pauses, scrolling — and captures a screenshot only when something actually changes. Each capture pairs a screenshot with the accessibility tree (the structured text the OS already knows about: buttons, labels, text fields). If accessibility data isn't available (e.g. remote desktops, games), it falls back to OCR. This gives you maximum data quality with minimal CPU and storage — no more processing thousands of identical frames.

Audio transcription

Captures system audio (what you hear) and microphone input (what you say). Real-time speech-to-text using OpenAI Whisper running locally on your device. Speaker identification and diarization. Works with any audio source — Zoom, Google Meet, Teams, or any other application.

AI-powered search

Natural language search across all OCR text and audio transcriptions. Filter by application name, window title, browser URL, date range. Semantic search using embeddings. Returns screenshots and audio clips alongside text results.

Timeline view

Visual timeline of your entire screen history. Scroll through your day like a DVR. Click any moment to see the full screenshot and extracted text. Play back audio from any time period.

Plugin system (Pipes)

Pipes are scheduled AI agents defined as markdown files. Each pipe is a pipe.md with a prompt and schedule — screenpipe runs an AI coding agent (like pi or claude-code) that queries your screen data, calls APIs, writes files, and takes actions. Built-in pipes include:

  • Obsidian sync: Automatically sync screen activity to Obsidian vault as daily logs
  • Reminders: Scan activity for todos and create Apple Reminders (macOS)
  • Idea tracker: Surface startup ideas from your browsing + market trends

Developers can create pipes by writing a markdown file in ~/.screenpipe/pipes/.

Pipe data permissions

Each pipe supports YAML frontmatter fields that give admins deterministic, OS-level control over what data AI agents can access:

  • App & window filtering: allow-apps, deny-apps, deny-windows (glob patterns)
  • Content type control: restrict to ocr, audio, input, or accessibility
  • Time & day restrictions: e.g. time-range: 09:00-18:00, days: Mon,Tue,Wed,Thu,Fri
  • Endpoint gating: allow-raw-sql: false, allow-frames: false

Enforced at three layers — skill gating (AI never learns denied endpoints), agent interception (blocked before execution), and server middleware (per-pipe cryptographic tokens). Not prompt-based. Deterministic.

MCP server (Model Context Protocol)

screenpipe runs as an MCP server, allowing AI assistants to query your screen history:

  • Works with Claude Desktop, Cursor, VS Code (Cline, Continue), and any MCP-compatible client
  • AI assistants can search your screen history, get recent context, and access meeting transcriptions
  • Zero configuration: claude mcp add screenpipe -- npx -y screenpipe-mcp

Developer API

Full REST API running on localhost (default port 3030). Endpoints for searching screen content, audio, frames. Raw SQL access to the underlying SQLite database. JavaScript/TypeScript SDK available.

Apple Intelligence integration (macOS)

On supported Macs, screenpipe uses Apple Intelligence for on-device AI processing — daily summaries, action items, and reminders with zero cloud dependency and zero cost.

Privacy and security

  • 100% local by default: All data stored on your device in a local SQLite database. Nothing sent to external servers.
  • Open source: MIT licensed, fully auditable codebase.
  • Local AI support: Use Ollama or any local model — no data sent to any cloud.
  • No account required: Core application works without any sign-up.
  • You own your data: Export, delete, or back up at any time.
  • Optional encrypted sync: End-to-end encrypted sync between devices (zero-knowledge encryption).
  • AI data permissions: Per-pipe YAML-based access control — deterministic enforcement at the OS level, not prompt-based. Three enforcement layers prevent AI agents from accessing unauthorized data.

How screenpipe compares to alternatives

Feature screenpipe Rewind / Limitless Microsoft Recall Granola
Open source ✅ MIT license ❌ ❌ ❌
Platforms macOS, Windows, Linux macOS, Windows Windows only macOS only
Data storage 100% local Cloud required Local (Windows) Cloud
Multi-monitor ✅ All monitors ❌ Active window only ✅ ❌ Meetings only
Audio transcription ✅ Local Whisper ✅ ❌ ✅ Cloud
Developer API ✅ Full REST API + SDK Limited ❌ ❌
Plugin system ✅ Pipes (AI agents) ❌ ❌ ❌
AI model choice Any (local or cloud) Proprietary Microsoft AI Proprietary
Team deployment ✅ Central config, AI permissions ❌ ❌ ❌
Pricing One-time purchase Subscription Bundled with Windows Subscription

Pricing

  • Lifetime: $400 one-time purchase. All features, all future updates, forever.
  • Lifetime + Pro 1 year: $600 one-time. Includes lifetime app + 1 year of Pro (cloud sync, priority support).
  • Pro subscription: $39/month for cloud sync between devices, priority support, and pro AI models.
  • Teams: Custom pricing. Shared configs, shared pipes, per-pipe AI data permissions, admin dashboard, MDM ready (Intune / SCCM). See screenpi.pe/team.

Integrations

  • AI coding assistants: Cursor, Claude Code, Cline, Continue, OpenCode, Gemini CLI
  • AI chat assistants: ChatGPT (via MCP), Claude Desktop (via MCP), any MCP-compatible client
  • Note-taking: Obsidian, Notion
  • Local AI: Ollama, any OpenAI-compatible model server
  • Automation: Custom pipes (scheduled AI agents as markdown files)

Teams & enterprise

screenpipe Teams lets organizations deploy AI agents across their team with full control over what AI can access. See screenpi.pe/team.

  • Central config management: Push capture settings (app filters, schedules, URL rules) to every device from an admin dashboard.
  • Shared pipes: Deploy AI workflows (auto-standups, meeting-to-tickets, time tracking) team-wide.
  • Per-pipe AI data permissions: YAML frontmatter controls what each pipe can access — apps, windows, content types, time ranges, endpoints. Enforced deterministically at the OS level via three layers (skill gating, agent interception, server middleware with per-pipe cryptographic tokens).
  • Privacy boundary: Admins control what gets captured and what AI accesses. They never see the actual data — everything stays on each employee's device.
  • Override rules: Employees can add stricter filters (e.g. also block personal email) but cannot weaken admin-set rules.
  • MDM ready: Deploy via Intune, SCCM, Robopack, or any MDM solution.
  • Enterprise: SSO/SAML, audit logs, SLA, SOC 2 / HIPAA compliance ready.

Technical architecture

  1. Event-driven capture: Listens for OS events (app switch, click, typing pause, scroll, clipboard). When something meaningful happens, captures a screenshot + accessibility tree together with the same timestamp. Falls back to OCR when accessibility data isn't available. Idle fallback captures periodically when nothing is happening.
  2. Audio processing: Whisper (local) or Deepgram (cloud) for speech-to-text. Speaker identification and diarization.
  3. Storage: Local SQLite with FTS5 full-text search. Screenshots saved as JPEGs on disk (~300 MB/8hr vs ~2 GB with continuous recording).
  4. API layer: REST API on localhost:3030. Search, frames, audio, elements, health, pipe management.
  5. Plugin layer: Pipes — scheduled AI agents as markdown files. Agent executes prompts with access to screenpipe API.
  6. UI layer: Desktop app built with Tauri (Rust + TypeScript).

API examples

Search screen content:

GET http://localhost:3030/search?q=meeting+notes&content_type=ocr&limit=10

Search audio transcriptions:

GET http://localhost:3030/search?q=budget+discussion&content_type=audio&limit=10

JavaScript SDK:

import { pipe } from "@screenpipe/js";

const results = await pipe.queryScreenpipe({
  q: "project deadline",
  contentType: "all",
  limit: 20,
  startTime: new Date(Date.now() - 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000).toISOString(),
});

Frequently asked questions

Is screenpipe free? The core engine is open source (MIT license). The desktop app is a one-time lifetime purchase ($400). No recurring subscription required for the core app.

Does screenpipe send my data to the cloud? No. All data is stored locally by default. You can use fully local AI models via Ollama for complete privacy.

How much disk space does it use? ~5–10 GB per month. Event-driven capture only stores frames when something changes, dramatically reducing storage compared to continuous recording.

Does it slow down my computer? Typical CPU usage is 5–10% on modern hardware. Event-driven capture only processes frames when something changes, and accessibility tree extraction is much lighter than OCR.

Can I use it with ChatGPT/Claude/Cursor? Yes. screenpipe runs as an MCP server, allowing Claude Desktop, Cursor, and other AI assistants to directly query your screen history.

Can it record multiple monitors? Yes. screenpipe captures all connected monitors simultaneously.

How does text extraction work? screenpipe primarily uses the OS accessibility tree to get structured text (buttons, labels, text fields) — this is faster and more accurate than OCR. When accessibility data isn't available (remote desktops, games, some Linux apps), it falls back to OCR: Apple Vision on macOS, Windows native OCR, or Tesseract on Linux.

Can I deploy screenpipe to my team? Yes. Screenpipe Teams provides central config management, shared AI pipes, and per-pipe data permissions. Admins control what gets captured and what AI can access — employees' actual data never leaves their devices. See screenpi.pe/team.

How do AI data permissions work? Each pipe supports YAML frontmatter fields (allow-apps, deny-apps, deny-windows, allow-content-types, time-range, days, allow-raw-sql, allow-frames) that deterministically control what data the AI agent can access. Enforcement happens at three OS-level layers — not by prompting the AI to behave. Even a compromised agent cannot access denied data.

Company

Built by screenpipe (formerly Mediar). Founded 2024. Based in San Francisco, CA.

  • Founder: Louis Beaumont (@louis030195)
  • Twitter: @screen_pipe
  • Email: louis@screenpi.pe
